severity_levels.HIGH: Material error that could cause incorrect regulatory decisions
severity_levels.MEDIUM: Missing detail or citation error that could mislead but doesn't change core requirement
severity_levels.LOW: Minor wording/numeric discrepancy with no practical impact

corrections.issue: Citation error: 6-month SPC extension attributed to Art. 37 of Reg 1901/2006
corrections.current_text: article field cites only 'Reg 1901/2006 Art. 37' for both the SPC extension and orphan exclusivity extension
corrections.correct_citation: SPC extension is in Art. 36(1) of Reg 1901/2006. Orphan exclusivity extension (10→12 years) is in Art. 37.
corrections.recommended_fix: Split into two sub-rules or update article field to 'Reg 1901/2006 Art. 36(1) and Art. 37' with clear attribution of which reward comes from which article.

corrections.issue: Missing the 4-year minimum university course duration from Art. 49(2)
corrections.current_text: States QP must hold diploma in specified disciplines with 2 years practical experience
corrections.correct_text: QP must hold diploma from a university course extending over at least 4 years of theoretical and practical study in specified disciplines, plus at least 2 years practical experience (Art. 49(2)-(3) of Dir 2001/83/EC).
corrections.recommended_fix: Add 'min_education_years: 4' field (already present in rule) and ensure the requirement text explicitly states 'at least four years of theoretical and practical study' per Art. 49(2).

corrections.issue: Incomplete article citation: references only Art. 51 but source cites Arts. 48, 49, and 51
corrections.current_text: 'Qualified Person(s) must be designated per Article 51 of Directive 2001/83/EC with specific education qualifications per Article 49'
corrections.correct_text: Should reference Articles 48, 49, and 51 of Directive 2001/83/EC as stated in Chapter 2, section 2.5.
corrections.recommended_fix: Update requirement text to cite all three articles: Art. 48 (obligation to have QP), Art. 49 (qualifications), Art. 51 (certification duties).

corrections.issue: Minor numeric discrepancy in Grade B >=5um in-operation particle limit
corrections.current_text: Extracted value appears as 2,900 in some representations
corrections.correct_text: Source Table 1 specifies 2,930 particles/m3 for Grade B >=5um in-operation
corrections.recommended_fix: Verify and correct the Grade B >=5um in-operation limit to 2,930 per Annex 1 Table 1. Also verify Grade A microbial limits against Table 6.

corrections.issue: Missing the 70-day PSUR submission timeline for intervals up to 12 months
corrections.current_text: States '90 calendar days for submission' without qualification
corrections.correct_text: Source specifies two distinct timelines: 70 calendar days for PSURs covering intervals up to 12 months; 90 calendar days for PSURs covering intervals exceeding 12 months.
corrections.recommended_fix: Update rule to include both timelines: 'PSUR data lock point shall coincide with IBD or EU birth date. Submission deadline: 70 calendar days for reporting intervals <=12 months; 90 calendar days for intervals >12 months.' This distinction is material for compliance tracking.

corrections.issue: Section reference may be incorrect (cited as II.B.3, may be II.C.2)
corrections.correct_text: The 7-day availability requirement appears in the operational procedures section (likely II.C.2), not in II.B.3 which covers PSMF content. Also, source emphasizes 'immediately upon request' with 7 days as the outer limit.
corrections.recommended_fix: Verify section reference against Module II PDF table of contents. Update to correct section number. Consider adding that the obligation is 'immediately upon request and at the latest within 7 calendar days.'

corrections.issue: Conflates two distinct 24-hour notification obligations with different subjects
corrections.correct_text: Two separate obligations exist: (1) Inter-authority coordination: Member States, Agency, and EC shall inform each other at least 24 hours in advance (DIR Art 106a(2)); (2) MAH embargo: MAH shall provide information to authorities at least 24 hours before any public safety announcement (DIR Art 106a, Section XV.C.1.3).
corrections.recommended_fix: Consider splitting into two rules (GVP-XV-003a and GVP-XV-003b) or adding a note clarifying the two distinct obligations and their different binding parties.

corrections.issue: Rule is UNVERIFIABLE — source PDF not available in the provided PDF set
corrections.current_text: RSABE for highly variable drugs: widening to max 69.84-143.19% permitted for Cmax ONLY, never for AUC
corrections.correct_text: Cannot verify. The EMA Bioequivalence Guideline (CPMP/EWP/QWP/1401/98 Rev.1) PDF is not included in the eu-gmp source folder.
corrections.recommended_fix: Obtain the EMA Bioequivalence Guideline PDF and add it to the source document set. Then re-verify this rule and the other EMA guideline rules (BE-001 through BE-007, BS-001 through BS-005, PV-001 through PV-007, EX-001 through EX-004, FDF-001 through FDF-004, IM-001 through IM-004). These EMA scientific guidelines are separate from GMP and should ideally be in their own source folder.

missing_source_documents.note: The following EMA scientific guidelines are cited in eu-gmp-rules.json but their source PDFs are not available in the provided folders. Rules referencing these documents could not be verified.
documents: EMA Finished Dosage Form Guideline EMA/362427/2017
missing_source_documents.recommendation: Create a dedicated source folder (e.g., training/stage-1/ema-guidelines/) and populate with these EMA scientific guideline PDFs for complete verification coverage.
